rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by aligning AGREE type declarations with expression types
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Remove invalid guarantees instead of adding architecture when references are unresolved
Use int for integer literals and arithmetic, real for real literals and arithmetic, and bool for comparison expressions
generic_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Write AGREE contracts inside annex agree blocks
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar declaration types are int, real, and bool
Use eq for helper declarations and assign for implementation-level bindings
Use const for named constants with concrete values
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not add architecture to fix unresolved references in guarantees; remove the invalid clause instead
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE diagnostics about unresolved property constants and definitions
AGREE diagnostics about missing EOF at 'annex'
AGREE diagnostics about unresolved unit literals
